office_does: The county council adopts policy and budgets and oversees county services, land use, infrastructure, and public health.
race_blurb: Incumbent Michelle Belkot faces public administrator Martin Pittioni and teacher John Zingale, with differences over tax and spending restraint, public investment, infrastructure, and services.

candidates.summary: A public-health and financial-regulation administrator advocating balanced growth, fiscal responsibility, public safety, and consensus.
highlights: Supports balanced growth, fiscal responsibility, public safety, and homelessness reduction [S1]
Cites work on mental-health licensure, prescription-history access, and professional-license portability [S1]

spending.basis: Combines fiscal responsibility and balanced budgets with support for behavioral-health and homelessness services.

experience.basis: Has substantial public regulatory and health administration but no documented elected county record.

candidates.summary: The incumbent councilor, prioritizing tax and spending restraint, police, fire, and infrastructure while opposing annexation, light-rail subsidies, and bridge tolls.
highlights: Opposes new taxes she considers unnecessary and places core infrastructure before program expansion [S1]
Prioritizes law enforcement and fire protection [S1]
Opposes unwanted annexation, Oregon light-rail subsidies, and bridge-related tolls [S1]

taxes.basis: Explicitly opposes new taxes and seeks to reduce taxes and housing costs.

spending.basis: Prioritizes core services before expansion and opposes taxpayer subsidies for Oregon light rail.

safety.basis: Makes law enforcement and fire protection explicit top priorities without a documented services-first alternative.

experience.basis: Incumbent county councilor with more than 20 years in federal acquisition and 25 years of military service.

candidates.summary: A teacher, union representative, and historic-preservation commissioner emphasizing social services, affordable housing, labor, rights, and natural-space protection.
highlights: Supports investment in social services, infrastructure, and affordable housing [S1]
Emphasizes natural-space protection, working families, and fundamental rights [S1]
Says county government should remain responsive and avoid wasting taxpayer money [S1]

spending.basis: Explicitly favors social-service and infrastructure investment as primary county tools.

housing.basis: Explicitly supports affordable housing alongside social-service investment, though the mechanism is unspecified.

climate.basis: Makes protection of natural spaces a stated county priority.

experience.basis: Brings commission, teaching, and union experience but no documented elected-government record.
